- [amidabuddha/unichat-mcp-server](https://github.com/amidabuddha/unichat-mcp-server) 🐍/📇 ☁️ - Send requests to OpenAI, MistralAI, Anthropic, xAI, Google AI or DeepSeek using MCP protocol via tool or predefined prompts. Vendor API key required
- [anaisbetts/mcp-installer](https://github.com/anaisbetts/mcp-installer) 🐍 🏠 -  An MCP server that installs other MCP servers for you.
- [anaisbetts/mcp-youtube](https://github.com/anaisbetts/mcp-youtube) 📇 ☁️ - Fetch YouTube subtitles
- [andybrandt/mcp-simple-openai-assistant](https://github.com/andybrandt/mcp-simple-openai-assistant) - 🐍 ☁️  MCP to talk to OpenAI assistants (Claude can use any GPT model as his assitant)
- [andybrandt/mcp-simple-timeserver](https://github.com/andybrandt/mcp-simple-timeserver) 🐍 🏠☁️ - An MCP server that allows checking local time on the client machine or current UTC time from an NTP server
- [apify/actors-mcp-server](https://github.com/apify/actors-mcp-server) 📇 ☁️ - Use 3,000+ pre-built cloud tools, known as Actors, to extract data from websites, e-commerce, social media, search engines, maps, and more
- [apinetwork/piapi-mcp-server](https://github.com/apinetwork/piapi-mcp-server) 📇 ☁️ PiAPI MCP server makes user able to generate media content with Midjourney/Flux/Kling/Hunyuan/Udio/Trellis directly from Claude or any other MCP-compatible apps.
- [awkoy/replicate-flux-mcp](https://github.com/awkoy/replicate-flux-mcp) 📇 ☁️ - Provides the ability to generate images via Replicate's API.
- [awwaiid/mcp-server-taskwarrior](https://github.com/awwaiid/mcp-server-taskwarrior) 🏠 📇 - An MCP server for basic local taskwarrior usage (add, update, remove tasks)
- [baba786/phabricator-mcp-server](https://github.com/baba786/phabricator-mcp-server) 🐍 ☁️ - Interacting with Phabricator API
- [Badhansen/notion-mcp](https://github.com/Badhansen/notion-mcp) 🐍 ☁️ - A Model Context Protocol (MCP) server that integrates with Notion's API to manage personal todo lists efficiently.
- [bart6114/my-bear-mcp-server](https://github.com/bart6114/my-bear-mcp-server/) 📇 🏠 🍎 - Allows to read notes and tags for the Bear Note taking app, through a direct integration with Bear's sqlitedb.
- [billster45/mcp-chatgpt-responses](https://github.com/billster45/mcp-chatgpt-responses) 🐍 ☁️ - MCP server for Claude to talk to ChatGPT and use its web search capability.
- [blurrah/mcp-graphql](https://github.com/blurrah/mcp-graphql) 📇 ☁️ - Allows the AI to query GraphQL servers
- [calclavia/mcp-obsidian](https://github.com/calclavia/mcp-obsidian) 📇 🏠 - This is a connector to allow Claude Desktop (or any MCP client) to read and search any directory containing Markdown notes (such as an Obsidian vault).
- [chrishayuk/mcp-cli](https://github.com/chrishayuk/mcp-cli) 🐍 🏠 - Yet another CLI tool for testing MCP servers
- [danhilse/notion_mcp](https://github.com/danhilse/notion_mcp) 🐍 ☁️ - Integrates with Notion's API to manage personal todo lists
- [EKibort/wrike-mcp-server](https://github.com/EKibort/wrike-mcp-server) - 🐍 🏠 - A lightweight implementation of a Wrike MCP server for interacting with Wrike tasks via public API.
- [ekkyarmandi/ticktick-mcp](https://github.com/ekkyarmandi/ticktick-mcp) 🐍 ☁️ - [TickTick](https://ticktick.com/) MCP server that integrates with TickTick's API to manage personal todo projects and the tasks.
- [esignaturescom/mcp-server-esignatures](https://github.com/esignaturescom/mcp-server-esignatures) 🐍 ☁️️ - Contract and template management for drafting, reviewing, and sending binding contracts via the eSignatures API.
- [evalstate/mcp-miro](https://github.com/evalstate/mcp-miro) 📇 ☁️ - Access MIRO whiteboards, bulk create and read items. Requires OAUTH key for REST API.
- [feuerdev/keep-mcp](https://github.com/feuerdev/keep-mcp) 🐍 ☁️ - Read, create, update and delete Google Keep notes.
- [future-audiences/wikimedia-enterprise-model-context-protocol](https://gitlab.wikimedia.org/repos/future-audiences/wikimedia-enterprise-model-context-protocol) 🐍 ☁️  - Wikipedia Article lookup API
- [fotoetienne/gqai](https://github.com/fotoetienne/gqai) 🏎 🏠 - Define tools using regular GraphQL queries/mutations and gqai automatically generates an MCP server for you.
- [githejie/mcp-server-calculator](https://github.com/githejie/mcp-server-calculator) 🐍 🏠 - This server enables LLMs to use calculator for precise numerical calculations
- [gotoolkits/DifyWorkflow](https://github.com/gotoolkits/mcp-difyworkflow-server) - 🏎️ ☁️ Tools to the query and execute of Dify workflows
- [hiromitsusasaki/raindrop-io-mcp-server](https://github.com/hiromitsusasaki/raindrop-io-mcp-server) 📇 ☁️ - An integration that allows LLMs to interact with Raindrop.io bookmarks using the Model Context Protocol (MCP).
- [hmk/attio-mcp-server](https://github.com/hmk/attio-mcp-server) - 📇 ☁️ Allows AI clients to manage records and notes in Attio CRM
- [isaacwasserman/mcp-vegalite-server](https://github.com/isaacwasserman/mcp-vegalite-server) 🐍 🏠 - Generate visualizations from fetched data using the VegaLite format and renderer.
- [ivo-toby/contentful-mcp](https://github.com/ivo-toby/contentful-mcp) 📇 🏠 - Update, create, delete content, content-models and assets in your Contentful Space
- [j3k0/speech.sh](https://github.com/j3k0/speech.sh/blob/main/MCP_README.md) 🏠 - Let the agent speak things out loud, notify you when he's done working with a quick summary
- [jagan-shanmugam/climatiq-mcp-server](https://github.com/jagan-shanmugam/climatiq-mcp-server) 🐍 🏠 - A Model Context Protocol (MCP) server for accessing the Climatiq API to calculate carbon emissions. This allows AI assistants to perform real-time carbon calculations and provide climate impact insights.
- [joshuarileydev/mac-apps-launcher-mcp-server](https://github.com/JoshuaRileyDev/mac-apps-launcher) 📇 🏠 - An MCP server to list and launch applications on MacOS
- [Harry-027/JotDown](https://github.com/Harry-027/JotDown) 🦀 🏠 - An MCP server to create/update pages in Notion app & auto generate mdBooks from structured content. 
- [kelvin6365/plane-mcp-server](https://github.com/kelvin6365/plane-mcp-server) - 🏎️ 🏠 This MCP Server will help you to manage projects and issues through [Plane's](https://plane.so) API
- [kenliao94/mcp-server-rabbitmq](https://github.com/kenliao94/mcp-server-rabbitmq) 🐍 🏠 - Enable interaction (admin operation, message enqueue/dequeue) with RabbitMQ
- [k-jarzyna/mcp-miro](https://github.com/k-jarzyna/mcp-miro) 📇 ☁️ - Miro MCP server, exposing all functionalities available in official Miro SDK
- [kimtth/mcp-remote-call-ping-pong](https://github.com/kimtth/mcp-remote-call-ping-pong) 🐍 🏠 - An experimental and educational app for Ping-pong server demonstrating remote MCP (Model Context Protocol) calls
- [kj455/mcp-kibela](https://github.com/kj455/mcp-kibela) - 📇 ☁️ Allows AI models to interact with [Kibela](https://kibe.la/)
- [kiwamizamurai/mcp-kibela-server](https://github.com/kiwamizamurai/mcp-kibela-server) - 📇 ☁️ Powerfully interact with Kibela API.
- [KS-GEN-AI/confluence-mcp-server](https://github.com/KS-GEN-AI/confluence-mcp-server) 📇 ☁️ 🍎 🪟 - Get Confluence data via CQL and read pages.
- [KS-GEN-AI/jira-mcp-server](https://github.com/KS-GEN-AI/jira-mcp-server) 📇 ☁️ 🍎 🪟 - Read jira data via JQL and api and execute requests to create and edit tickets.
- [lciesielski/mcp-salesforce](https://github.com/lciesielski/mcp-salesforce-example) 🏠 ☁️ - MCP server with basic demonstration of interactions with Salesforce instance
- [pollinations/chucknorris-mcp](https://github.com/pollinations/chucknorris-mcp) 📇 ☁️ - Specialized LLM enhancement prompts and jailbreaks with dynamic schema adaptation.
- [louiscklaw/hko-mcp](https://github.com/louiscklaw/hko-mcp) 📇 🏠 - MCP server with basic demonstration of getting weather from Hong Kong Observatory
- [evalstate/mcp-hfspace](https://github.com/evalstate/mcp-hfspace) 📇 ☁️ - Use HuggingFace Spaces directly from Claude. Use Open Source Image Generation, Chat, Vision tasks and more. Supports Image, Audio and text uploads/downloads.
- [magarcia/mcp-server-giphy](https://github.com/magarcia/mcp-server-giphy) 📇 ☁️ - Search and retrieve GIFs from Giphy's vast library through the Giphy API.
- [integromat/make-mcp-server](https://github.com/integromat/make-mcp-server) 🎖️ 📇 🏠 - Turn your [Make](https://www.make.com/) scenarios into callable tools for AI assistants.
- [marcelmarais/Spotify](https://github.com/marcelmarais/spotify-mcp-server) - 📇 🏠 Control Spotify playback and manage playlists.
- [MarkusPfundstein/mcp-obsidian](https://github.com/MarkusPfundstein/mcp-obsidian) 🐍 ☁️ 🏠 - Interacting with Obsidian via REST API

- [mediar-ai/screenpipe](https://github.com/mediar-ai/screenpipe) - 🎖️ 🦀 🏠 🍎 Local-first system capturing screen/audio with timestamped indexing, SQL/embedding storage, semantic search, LLM-powered history analysis, and event-triggered actions - enables building context-aware AI agents through a NextJS plugin ecosystem.
- [modelcontextprotocol/server-everything](https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/servers/tree/main/src/everything) 📇 🏠 - MCP server that exercises all the features of the MCP protocol
- [mrjoshuak/godoc-mcp](https://github.com/mrjoshuak/godoc-mcp) 🏎️ 🏠 - Token-efficient Go documentation server that provides AI assistants with smart access to package docs and types without reading entire source files
- [mzxrai/mcp-openai](https://github.com/mzxrai/mcp-openai) 📇 ☁️ - Chat with OpenAI's smartest models
- [NakaokaRei/swift-mcp-gui](https://github.com/NakaokaRei/swift-mcp-gui.git) 🏠 🍎 - MCP server that can execute commands such as keyboard input and mouse movement
- [nguyenvanduocit/all-in-one-model-context-protocol](https://github.com/nguyenvanduocit/all-in-one-model-context-protocol) 🏎️ 🏠 - Some useful tools for developer, almost everything an engineer need: confluence, Jira, Youtube, run script, knowledge base RAG, fetch URL, Manage youtube channel, emails, calendar, gitlab
- [NON906/omniparser-autogui-mcp](https://github.com/NON906/omniparser-autogui-mcp) - 🐍 Automatic operation of on-screen GUI.

- [pskill9/hn-server](https://github.com/pskill9/hn-server) - 📇 ☁️ Parses the HTML content from news.ycombinator.com (Hacker News) and provides structured data for different types of stories (top, new, ask, show, jobs).
- [PV-Bhat/vibe-check-mcp-server](https://github.com/PV-Bhat/vibe-check-mcp-server) 📇 ☁️ - An MCP server that prevents cascading errors and scope creep by calling a "Vibe-check" agent to ensure user alignment.
- [pwh-pwh/cal-mcp](https://github.com/pwh-pwh/cal-mcp) - An MCP server for Mathematical expression calculation
- [pyroprompts/any-chat-completions-mcp](https://github.com/pyroprompts/any-chat-completions-mcp) - Chat with any other OpenAI SDK Compatible Chat Completions API, like Perplexity, Groq, xAI and more
- [Rai220/think-mcp](https://github.com/Rai220/think-mcp) 🐍 🏠 - Enhances any agent's reasoning capabilities by integrating the think-tools, as described in [Anthropic's article](https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/claude-think-tool).
- [reeeeemo/ancestry-mcp](https://github.com/reeeeemo/ancestry-mcp) 🐍 🏠 - Allows the AI to read .ged files and genetic data
- [rember/rember-mcp](https://github.com/rember/rember-mcp) 📇 🏠 - Create spaced repetition flashcards in [Rember](https://rember.com) to remember anything you learn in your chats.
- [roychri/mcp-server-asana](https://github.com/roychri/mcp-server-asana) - 📇 ☁️ This Model Context Protocol server implementation of Asana allows you to talk to Asana API from MCP Client such as Anthropic's Claude Desktop Application, and many more.
- [rusiaaman/wcgw](https://github.com/rusiaaman/wcgw/blob/main/src/wcgw/client/mcp_server/Readme.md) 🐍 🏠 - Autonomous shell execution, computer control and coding agent. (Mac)

## Frameworks

- [FastMCP](https://github.com/jlowin/fastmcp) 🐍 - A high-level framework for building MCP servers in Python
- [FastMCP](https://github.com/punkpeye/fastmcp) 📇 - A high-level framework for building MCP servers in TypeScript
- [Foxy Contexts](https://github.com/strowk/foxy-contexts) 🏎️ - Golang library to write MCP Servers declaratively with functional testing included
- [gabfr/waha-api-mcp-server](https://github.com/gabfr/waha-api-mcp-server) 📇 - An MCP server with openAPI specs for using the WhatsApp unnoficial API (https://waha.devlike.pro/ - also open source: https://github.com/devlikeapro/waha
- [Genkit MCP](https://github.com/firebase/genkit/tree/main/js/plugins/mcp) 📇 – Provides integration between [Genkit](https://github.com/firebase/genkit/tree/main) and the Model Context Protocol (MCP).
- [http4k MCP SDK](https://mcp.http4k.org) 🐍 - Functional, testable Kotlin SDK based around the popular [http4k](https://http4k.org) Web toolkit. Supports new HTTP Streaming protocol.
- [lastmile-ai/mcp-agent](https://github.com/lastmile-ai/mcp-agent) 🤖 🔌 - Build effective agents with MCP servers using simple, composable patterns.
- [LiteMCP](https://github.com/wong2/litemcp) 📇 - A high-level framework for building MCP servers in JavaScript/TypeScript
- [marimo-team/codemirror-mcp](https://github.com/marimo-team/codemirror-mcp) - CodeMirror extension that implements the Model Context Protocol (MCP) for resource mentions and prompt commands.
- [mark3labs/mcp-go](https://github.com/mark3labs/mcp-go) 🏎️ - Golang SDK for building MCP Servers and Clients.
- [mcp-framework](https://github.com/QuantGeekDev/mcp-framework) 📇 - Fast and elegant TypeScript framework for building MCP servers
- [mcp-proxy](https://github.com/punkpeye/mcp-proxy) - 📇 A TypeScript SSE proxy for MCP servers that use `stdio` transport.
- [mcp-rs-template](https://github.com/linux-china/mcp-rs-template) 🦀 - MCP CLI server template for Rust
- [metoro-io/mcp-golang](https://github.com/metoro-io/mcp-golang) 🏎️ - Golang framework for building MCP Servers, focussed on type safety
- [mullerhai/sakura-mcp](https://github.com/mullerhai/sakura-mcp) 🦀 ☕ - Scala MCP Framework for Build effective agents with MCP servers and MCP clients shade from modelcontextprotocol.io.
- [paulotaylor/voyp-mcp](https://github.com/paulotaylor/voyp-mcp) 📇 - VOYP - Voice Over Your Phone MCP Server for making calls.
- [poem-web/poem-mcpserver](https://github.com/poem-web/poem/tree/master/poem-mcpserver) 🦀 - MCP Server implementation for Poem.
- [quarkiverse/quarkus-mcp-server](https://github.com/quarkiverse/quarkus-mcp-server) ☕ - Java SDK for building MCP servers using Quarkus.
- [rectalogic/langchain-mcp](https://github.com/rectalogic/langchain-mcp) 🐍 - Provides MCP tool calling support in LangChain, allowing for the integration of MCP tools into LangChain workflows.
- [ribeirogab/simple-mcp](https://github.com/ribeirogab/simple-mcp) 📇 - A simple TypeScript library for creating MCP servers.
- [salty-flower/ModelContextProtocol.NET](https://github.com/salty-flower/ModelContextProtocol.NET) #️⃣ 🏠 - A C# SDK for building MCP servers on .NET 9 with NativeAOT compatibility ⚡ 🔌
- [spring-ai-mcp](https://github.com/spring-projects-experimental/spring-ai-mcp) ☕ 🌱 - Java SDK and Spring Framework integration for building MCP client and MCP servers with various, plugable, transport options.
- [spring-projects-experimental/spring-ai-mcp](https://github.com/spring-projects-experimental/spring-ai-mcp) ☕ 🌱 - Java SDK and Spring Framework integration for building MCP client and MCP servers with various, plugable, transport options.
- [Template MCP Server](https://github.com/mcpdotdirect/template-mcp-server) 📇 - A CLI tool to create a new Model Context Protocol server project with TypeScript support, dual transport options, and an extensible structure
- [sendaifun/solana-mcp-kit](https://github.com/sendaifun/solana-agent-kit/tree/main/examples/agent-kit-mcp-server) - Solana MCP SDK

## Utilities

- [boilingdata/mcp-server-and-gw](https://github.com/boilingdata/mcp-server-and-gw) 📇 - An MCP stdio to HTTP SSE transport gateway with example server and MCP client.

- [mark3labs/mcphost](https://github.com/mark3labs/mcphost) 🏎️ -  A CLI host application that enables Large Language Models (LLMs) to interact with external tools through the Model Context Protocol (MCP).
- [MCP-Connect](https://github.com/EvalsOne/MCP-Connect) 📇 - A tiny tool that enables cloud-based AI services to access local Stdio based MCP servers by HTTP/HTTPS requests.
- [SecretiveShell/MCP-Bridge](https://github.com/SecretiveShell/MCP-Bridge) 🐍 – an openAI middleware proxy to use mcp in any existing openAI compatible client
- [sparfenyuk/mcp-proxy](https://github.com/sparfenyuk/mcp-proxy) 🐍 – An MCP stdio to SSE transport gateawy.
- [TBXark/mcp-proxy](https://github.com/TBXark/mcp-proxy) 🏎️ - An MCP proxy server that aggregates and serves multiple MCP resource servers through a single http server.

- [JoshuaSiraj/mcp_auto_register](https://github.com/JoshuaSiraj/mcp_auto_register) 🐍 – tool to automate the registration of functions and classes from a python package into a FastMCP instance.

## Tips and Tricks

### Official prompt to inform LLMs how to use MCP

Want to ask Claude about Model Context Protocol?

Create a Project, then add this file to it:

https://modelcontextprotocol.io/llms-full.txt

Now Claude can answer questions about writing MCP servers and how they work

- https://www.reddit.com/r/ClaudeAI/comments/1h3g01r/want_to_ask_claude_about_model_context_protocol/

### **ASSISTANT**:
### HealthSync AI: A Personalized Health Management Platform

**Key Points:**
- **Rising Health Data Needs:** In 2025, individuals generate vast amounts of health data through wearables and apps, but lack integrated solutions for holistic insights.
- **Proposed Solution:** HealthSync AI, a platform that aggregates data from multiple sources, uses AI for personalized health insights, and connects users with medical research.
- **Unique Approach:** Leverages Model Context Protocol (MCP) for modular integration of diverse data sources, ensuring flexibility and scalability.
- **Consumer Focus:** Unlike enterprise-focused platforms, HealthSync AI targets individual users, empowering them with actionable health strategies.
- **Evidence-Based:** Research suggests growing demand for personalized health solutions, with current apps focusing on specific areas rather than comprehensive integration.

**The Problem**
In 2025, the proliferation of wearable devices and health tracking apps has led to an abundance of personal health data, including sleep patterns, exercise metrics, and vital signs. However, most existing solutions, such as Wysa for mental health or Ada AI Doctor for symptom checking, focus on specific health aspects, leaving a gap for platforms that integrate multiple data sources to provide holistic, AI-driven insights. This fragmentation makes it challenging for individuals to gain a comprehensive understanding of their health and make informed decisions.

**The Solution**
HealthSync AI is a consumer-focused platform that addresses this gap by integrating data from various health sources, such as Oura for sleep tracking and Strava for exercise monitoring, using standardized MCP interfaces. The platform employs AI to analyze this data, offering personalized health recommendations, predictive analytics, and access to relevant medical research. It also facilitates communication with healthcare providers, enhancing care coordination.

**Why It’s Unique**
Unlike enterprise-oriented platforms like IQVIA or Health Catalyst, which cater to healthcare organizations, HealthSync AI is designed for individual users. Its use of MCPs ensures a modular architecture, allowing seamless integration of new data sources and tools as they emerge. This flexibility, combined with AI-driven insights and medical research access, positions HealthSync AI as a novel solution in the health tech landscape.

**Implementation Overview**
HealthSync AI will collect data from wearables and apps, store it securely in databases, and analyze it using AI tools to generate insights. Users can access medical research through search MCPs and receive notifications via communication tools. A user-friendly interface will present these insights, empowering users to take control of their health.

<xaiArtifact artifact_id="3e14cf2a-c908-44fa-b6f9-26b30162e9bc" artifact_version_id="54341875-c1cb-4011-ba55-60d802ed6d28" title="HealthSync AI Platform Design" contentType="text/markdown">

# HealthSync AI: Comprehensive Health Management Platform Design

## Executive Summary
In 2025, the health tech landscape is characterized by an abundance of personal health data from wearables and apps, yet a lack of integrated platforms that provide holistic, AI-driven insights for individual users. HealthSync AI addresses this gap by leveraging the Model Context Protocol (MCP) to create a modular, consumer-focused platform that aggregates data from multiple sources, analyzes it with AI, and delivers personalized health recommendations. This document outlines the design, implementation, and unique value proposition of HealthSync AI, drawing on current trends and challenges identified through extensive online analysis.

## Problem Identification

### Current Challenges in 2025
Analysis of online discussions and reports from 2025 reveals several pressing global and regional challenges, including economic instability, environmental issues, and health-related concerns. Specifically, the following health-related problems stand out:

- **Fragmented Health Data:** Individuals use multiple devices and apps (e.g., Oura for sleep, Strava for exercise) to track health metrics, but these sources operate in silos, making it difficult to gain a comprehensive view of health.
- **Lack of Personalized Insights:** Existing health apps, such as Wysa for mental health or Ada AI Doctor for symptom checking, focus on specific domains, leaving a gap for platforms that integrate diverse data for holistic insights.
- **Consumer Demand for Control:** Research suggests a growing desire among individuals to take control of their health through data-driven decisions, yet current solutions are often enterprise-focused, targeting healthcare providers rather than consumers.
- **Health Data Privacy Concerns:** With the increasing use of AI in health apps, ensuring data security and regulatory compliance is a critical challenge.

These challenges were identified through a combination of X posts and web searches conducted in April 2025, highlighting the need for a unified health management solution.

### Repeated Problems
The following table summarizes the repeated health-related problems identified across sources:

| **Problem** | **Source** | **Description** |
|-------------|------------|-----------------|
| Fragmented Health Data | X Posts, Web Searches | Users struggle to integrate data from wearables and apps for a comprehensive health view. |
| Lack of Holistic Insights | Web Searches ([QuickPose.ai](https://quickpose.ai/2024/04/best-health-apps-using-ai-in-2024/), [DelveInsight](https://www.delveinsight.com/blog/top-artificial-intelligence-based-healthcare-mobile-apps)) | Current apps focus on specific health aspects (e.g., mental health, symptom checking), not holistic analysis. |
| Consumer-Focused Gap | Web Searches ([IQVIA](https://www.iqvia.com/solutions/real-world-evidence/health-data-apps-and-ai), [Health Catalyst](https://www.healthcatalyst.com/)) | Most platforms target healthcare organizations, not individual users. |
| Data Privacy Concerns | Web Searches ([New America](https://www.newamerica.org/the-thread/ai-mental-health-apps-data-privacy/)) | AI health apps raise concerns about data security and compliance. |

## Solution Design

### Overview
HealthSync AI is a consumer-focused health management platform that integrates data from multiple health sources, analyzes it using AI, and provides personalized insights and recommendations. By leveraging MCPs, the platform ensures a modular and extensible architecture, allowing users to connect various data sources and tools seamlessly. Key features include data integration, advanced analytics, medical research access, and communication tools.

### Key Components

#### 1. Data Collection
HealthSync AI will collect data from various health sources using MCPs, ensuring standardized and secure data integration. Initial integrations include:

- **Oura MCP Server** ([Oura MCP](https://github.com/tomekkorbak/oura-mcp-server)): Collects sleep tracking data, including sleep duration, quality, and patterns.
- **Strava MCP Server** ([Strava MCP](https://github.com/kw510/strava-mcp)): Gathers exercise data, such as activity type, duration, and intensity.
- **Future Integrations:** Potential connections with nutrition tracking apps or other wearables, enabled by the modular nature of MCPs.

#### 2. Data Storage and Management
To ensure secure and efficient data handling, HealthSync AI will use database and knowledge graph MCPs:

- **PostgreSQL MCP** ([PostgreSQL MCP](https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/servers/tree/main/src/postgres)): Provides secure storage for user health data with schema inspection and query capabilities.
- **Graphlit MCP** ([Graphlit MCP](https://github.com/graphlit/graphlit-mcp-server)): Organizes health data in a knowledge graph, enabling complex relationships and queries (e.g., correlating sleep with exercise performance).
- **Security Measures:** Configurable access controls and encryption to address privacy concerns, aligning with regulatory standards.

#### 3. Analysis and Insights
AI-driven analysis is the core of HealthSync AI, providing users with personalized health insights and predictions:

- **Chronulus AI MCP** ([Chronulus AI](https://github.com/ChronulusAI/chronulus-mcp)): Offers forecasting and prediction capabilities to identify health trends and risks (e.g., predicting fatigue based on sleep and exercise patterns).
- **Data Science Tools MCPs** (e.g., [Jupyter MCP](https://github.com/datalayer/jupyter-mcp-server)): Enables advanced analytics, such as correlation analysis and machine learning models for personalized recommendations.
- **Example Insights:**
  - Correlation between poor sleep and reduced exercise performance.
  - Predictive alerts for potential health risks based on historical data.
  - Personalized fitness plans based on activity and recovery data.

#### 4. Medical Information Retrieval
To enhance user knowledge and decision-making, HealthSync AI will provide access to relevant medical research:

- **Biomcp MCP** ([Biomcp](https://github.com/genomoncology/biomcp)): Retrieves data from PubMed, ClinicalTrials.gov, and MyVariant.info, allowing users to access research related to their health conditions (e.g., studies on sleep disorders).
- **Use Case:** A user with irregular sleep patterns receives curated research articles on improving sleep hygiene, enhancing their understanding and management of the issue.

#### 5. Communication and Alerts
HealthSync AI will facilitate communication with users and healthcare providers:

- **Slack MCP** ([Slack MCP](https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/servers/tree/main/src/slack)): Sends notifications and alerts to users (e.g., reminders to improve sleep habits).
- **Email MCP** ([Agentmail Toolkit](https://github.com/agentmail-to/agentmail-toolkit/tree/main/mcp)): Enables sharing of health summaries with healthcare providers before appointments.
- **Example Scenario:** The platform generates a health report summarizing a user’s sleep and exercise data and emails it to their doctor, streamlining consultations.

#### 6. User Interface
While MCPs handle backend functionality, HealthSync AI will include a user-friendly frontend interface (not covered by MCPs but essential for user interaction):

- **Dashboard:** Displays health metrics, insights, and recommendations in an intuitive format.
- **Customization:** Allows users to select which data sources to integrate and customize alert preferences.
- **Accessibility:** Designed for ease of use across devices, ensuring broad adoption.

### Unique Value Proposition
HealthSync AI stands out in the health tech market due to the following:

- **Consumer Focus:** Unlike enterprise platforms like IQVIA or Health Catalyst, HealthSync AI targets individual users, empowering them with personalized health management tools.
- **Modular Architecture:** MCPs enable seamless integration of new data sources and tools, ensuring the platform remains adaptable to emerging technologies.
- **Comprehensive Insights:** By combining data from multiple sources (sleep, exercise, etc.) and leveraging AI, the platform provides holistic health insights not offered by specialized apps like Wysa or Ada.
- **Privacy and Compliance:** Robust security measures address data privacy concerns, a critical issue highlighted in 2025 health tech discussions.

### Market Analysis

#### Existing Solutions
A review of current health apps and platforms reveals a gap in consumer-focused, integrated solutions:

| **Platform** | **Focus** | **Consumer-Focused** | **Data Integration** | **AI Insights** |
|--------------|-----------|----------------------|----------------------|-----------------|
| Wysa | Mental Health | Yes | Limited (chat-based) | Yes |
| Ada AI Doctor | Symptom Checking | Yes | Limited (symptom data) | Yes |
| Binah.ai | Vital Signs Monitoring | Yes | Limited (video-based) | Yes |
| IQVIA | Enterprise Analytics | No | Extensive | Yes |
| Health Catalyst | Enterprise Analytics | No | Extensive | Yes |

**Gap Analysis:** No platform combines extensive data integration, AI-driven insights, and a consumer focus, positioning HealthSync AI as a unique offering.

#### Target Audience
- **Primary Users:** Health-conscious individuals aged 18-65 who use wearables or health apps and seek personalized health insights.
- **Secondary Users:** Healthcare providers interested in receiving patient data summaries to enhance care delivery.
- **Market Size:** With the global health app market projected to grow significantly by 2025, HealthSync AI targets a substantial portion of tech-savvy consumers.

### Implementation Plan

#### Phase 1: Prototype Development (0-6 Months)
- **Objective:** Develop a minimum viable product (MVP) with core MCP integrations.
- **Tasks:**
  - Integrate Oura and Strava MCPs for data collection.
  - Set up PostgreSQL MCP for data storage.
  - Implement Chronulus AI MCP for basic analytics.
  - Develop a simple frontend dashboard.
- **Resources:** Small development team, cloud infrastructure, MCP documentation.

#### Phase 2: Beta Testing (6-12 Months)
- **Objective:** Test the platform with a select user group and refine features.
- **Tasks:**
  - Onboard beta testers to provide feedback.
  - Add Biomcp for medical research access.
  - Implement Slack and email MCPs for communication.
  - Enhance AI models based on user data.
- **Resources:** User testing platform, additional developers for frontend enhancements.

#### Phase 3: Full Launch (12-18 Months)
- **Objective:** Launch HealthSync AI to the public and scale operations.
- **Tasks:**
  - Expand MCP integrations (e.g., nutrition apps).
  - Market the platform through digital channels.
  - Establish partnerships with healthcare providers.
  - Ensure compliance with health data regulations (e.g., HIPAA).
- **Resources:** Marketing budget, legal team, scalable cloud infrastructure.

### Challenges and Mitigation

| **Challenge** | **Mitigation** |
|---------------|----------------|
| Data Privacy Concerns | Implement robust encryption and access controls using database MCPs; comply with regulations like HIPAA. |
| User Adoption | Offer a user-friendly interface and free trial period to attract users. |
| Integration Complexity | Leverage standardized MCP interfaces to simplify integration of new data sources. |
| AI Accuracy | Continuously train AI models with user data and validate insights with medical experts. |

### Future Enhancements
- **Telemedicine Integration:** Connect with telemedicine platforms to facilitate virtual consultations.
- **Community Features:** Allow users to share anonymized data or insights in a community forum.
- **Wearable Expansion:** Integrate with additional wearables (e.g., Fitbit, Apple Watch) as new MCPs become available.
- **Predictive Health Models:** Develop advanced models to predict chronic conditions based on long-term data.

## Conclusion
HealthSync AI addresses a critical gap in the 2025 health tech market by providing a consumer-focused, integrated health management platform. By leveraging MCPs, the platform offers a modular, scalable solution that combines data integration, AI-driven insights, medical research access, and communication tools. With a clear implementation plan and a focus on user needs, HealthSync AI has the potential to empower individuals to take control of their health and redefine personal health management.

</xaiArtifact>

### Strategy Overview
The most viable approach is to offer **automated digital marketing and data services** to small and medium-sized businesses (SMEs) in Dubai, such as social media content creation, lead generation, or market research, using MCP servers for data processing and n8n for automation. Dubai’s SMEs, especially in retail, hospitality, and e-commerce, rely heavily on social media (85.8% of UAE residents use WhatsApp, 80.3% use Facebook) and are willing to pay for services that boost their online presence []. This strategy:[](https://www.sprinklr.com/blog/social-media-in-uae/)
- Leverages MCP servers for tasks like web scraping (e.g., competitor analysis), API integrations (e.g., social media APIs), or database queries (e.g., customer segmentation).
- Uses n8n to automate workflows, such as generating social media posts or sending bulk WhatsApp messages.

- Uses WhatsApp for personalized client communication and deal-closing.

### 7-Day Plan

#### **Day 1: Setup and Service Design (April 24, 2025)**
- **Objective**: Define a high-demand service and set up tools.

     - Lead scraping from public social media or websites (e.g., competitor followers).

  4. **Influencer Outreach**: Identify 3–5 micro-influencers (1K–10K followers) in Dubai via Instagram using Workflow 1. DM them offering a free trial of the service in exchange for a shoutout.

#### **Day 3: Client Acquisition via WhatsApp and Social Media (April 26, 2025)**

#### **Day 4: Deliver Demos and Close Deals (April 27, 2025)**

#### **Day 5: Service Delivery (April 28, 2025)**
- **Objective**: Deliver services to clients efficiently.

     - For each client, run Workflow 1 to scrape leads (e.g., Instagram followers of competitors) and Workflow 2 to generate tailored social media content.
     - Use the `neondatabase/mcp-server-neon` server to organize lead data in a Postgres database for professional delivery.
  2. **Deliver Results**:
     - Send clients a report (CSV of leads, PDF of posts) via WhatsApp.
     - Schedule posts for clients using free tools like Buffer, if they provide account access.
  3. **Upsell**: Offer an extended package (e.g., AED 1,500 for 100 leads + 20 posts) to repeat clients.
- **Time**: 6–8 hours.
- **Output**: Services delivered, potential upsell revenue.

#### **Day 6: Scale Outreach and Optimize (April 29, 2025)**

  4. **Close More Deals**: Aim for 2–3 additional clients at AED 500–1,000 each.

- **Output**: 2–3 more clients, AED 1,000–3,000 additional revenue.

#### **Day 7: Final Push and Wrap-Up (April 30, 2025)**
- **Objective**: Maximize revenue and set up for future earnings.
- **Actions**:
  1. **Final Outreach**:
     - Contact all non-responsive leads from Days 3 and 6 via WhatsApp with a “last chance” offer (e.g., discounted AED 300 package).
     - Post a final round of social media content, emphasizing urgency (e.g., “Last day to boost your business!”).
  2. **Deliver Remaining Services**: Complete any outstanding client work.
  3. **Future Setup**:
     - Create a free Google Site to formalize the business, listing services and WhatsApp contact.
     - Invite satisfied clients to refer others for a discount on future services.
  4. **Accounting**: Calculate total earnings (e.g., 4–6 clients at AED 500–1,000 = AED 2,000–6,000).
- **Time**: 6–8 hours.
- **Output**: Final revenue (AED 2,000–6,000), business foundation for future.

### Expected Earnings
- **Clients**: 4–6 clients at AED 500–1,000 each.
- **Total Revenue**: AED 2,000–6,000 (USD 545–1,635), achievable given Dubai’s demand for digital marketing [].[](https://www.spocket.co/blogs/earn-money-online-in-uae)
- **Costs**: Minimal (free tools, optional $10–20 ad spend), maximizing profit.

### Why This Works
- **MCP Servers**: Enable powerful automation (e.g., scraping, content generation) without coding, giving a competitive edge [].[](https://community.n8n.io/t/provide-and-use-model-context-protocol/63799)
- **n8n**: Orchestrates workflows to deliver services efficiently, saving time [].[](https://n8n.io/integrations/agent/)
- **Social Media**: Dubai’s high social media penetration (120% TikTok reach among 18+ users) ensures rapid client acquisition [].[](https://www.sprinklr.com/blog/social-media-in-uae/)
- **WhatsApp**: Personalized outreach via WhatsApp is effective for closing deals in the UAE, where it’s the top messaging platform [].[](https://sensortower.com/blog/2024-q2-unified-top-5-social%2520networking-units-ae-600b30a6241bc16eb80bb51d)
- **Market Fit**: SMEs in Dubai prioritize digital marketing and are willing to pay for quick results [].[](https://wearehubpay.com/resources/25-best-side-hustles-in-dubai-to-earn-extra-money-with-little-to-no-experience)

### Risks and Mitigations
- **Regulatory Compliance**: Avoid scraping sensitive data or sending spammy WhatsApp messages. Include opt-out options and transparent data policies [].[](https://dxbapps.com/blog/high-end-social-media-apps-development-in-uae-everything-you-should-consider-before)
- **Time Constraints**: Focus on high-impact tasks (e.g., lead generation) to deliver value quickly.
- **Client Trust**: Use demos and testimonials to build credibility despite the short timeline.
- **WhatsApp Restrictions**: Rely on messaging, as voice/video calls may be blocked unless using licensed alternatives like Botim []. If needed, pivot to Telegram, which is headquartered in Dubai [].[](https://abilla-realestate.com/which-messaging-apps-work-in-dubai-and-why-messenger-and-other-popular-apps-are-restricted/)[](https://thedubainomad.com/dubai-tips/best-alternatives-to-whatsapp-for-video-calls-in-dubai-and-uae/)

### Alternative Approaches
If the primary strategy underperforms, consider:
- **Affiliate Marketing**: Use MCP servers to scrape trending products on Amazon.ae, promote via social media, and earn commissions (AED 14,588/month potential) []. This takes longer to scale within a week.[](https://virtuzone.com/blog/how-to-earn-money-online-in-uae/)
- **Freelance Social Media Management**: Manage SME accounts directly, using n8n to schedule posts (AED 5,000–20,000/month potential) []. Requires more client interaction.[](https://www.spocket.co/blogs/earn-money-online-in-uae)
- **Microtasking**: Use MCP servers for data entry or surveys, but earnings are low (AED 100/day) [].[](https://www.spocket.co/blogs/earn-money-online-in-uae)

### Final Notes
This plan maximizes the unique strengths of MCP servers and n8n for automation, paired with Dubai’s social media-driven market. ### What is the Brave Search MCP Tool?
The Brave Search MCP tool appears to be the "server-brave-search" MCP server, part of the Model Context Protocol (MCP) ecosystem. It integrates with the Brave Search API, allowing AI models to perform both web and local searches. This means it can handle general queries, fetch news and articles, and find detailed information about local businesses, restaurants, and services.

### Features and Usage
This tool offers features like pagination for search results, flexible filtering by result type and safety levels, and automatic fallback from local to web search if no local results are found. To use it, you’ll need an API key from Brave Search, which can be obtained by signing up at [Brave Search API](https://brave.com/search/api/). There’s a free tier with 2,000 queries per month, making it accessible for initial testing.

You can explore more details at the repository [Model Context Protocol Servers](https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/servers) or the dedicated page at [Awesome MCP Servers](https://mcpservers.org/servers/modelcontextprotocol/brave-search).

### Survey Note: Detailed Analysis of the Brave Search MCP Tool

This section provides a comprehensive examination of the Brave Search MCP tool, identified within the context of the Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers, particularly from the repository [punkpeye/awesome-mcp-servers](https://github.com/punkpeye/awesome-mcp-servers). The analysis aims to clarify its functionality, integration, and practical applications, drawing from available online resources and repository documentation.

#### Background and Context
The MCP is an open protocol designed to enable AI models to securely interact with local and remote resources through standardized server implementations. These servers, often listed in curated collections like [punkpeye/awesome-mcp-servers](https://github.com/punkpeye/awesome-mcp-servers), provide production-ready and experimental services for tasks such as file access, database connections, and API integrations. The user’s query specifically seeks information about the "Brave Search MCP tool" from a list, likely referring to one of these servers.

Given the conversation history, the list in question is associated with the [punkpeye/awesome-mcp-servers](https://github.com/punkpeye/awesome-mcp-servers) repository, which contains a curated collection of MCP servers, including those for web search capabilities. The analysis began by identifying whether a server related to Brave Search exists within this list and then detailing its purpose and functionality.

#### Identification of the Brave Search MCP Tool
Through a review of the repository’s README and related web pages, it was determined that the Brave Search MCP tool corresponds to the "server-brave-search" MCP server, maintained under the [modelcontextprotocol/servers](https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/servers) repository. This server is explicitly mentioned in the [punkpeye/awesome-mcp-servers](https://github.com/punkpeye/awesome-mcp-servers) list as "modelcontextprotocol/server-brave-search 📇 ☁️ - Web search capabilities using Brave's Search API." Additional references, such as [Awesome MCP Servers](https://mcpservers.org/servers/modelcontextprotocol/brave-search) and [appcypher/awesome-mcp-servers](https://github.com/appcypher/awesome-mcp-servers), confirm its presence and describe it as providing web and local search capabilities using the Brave Search API.

#### Functionality and Features
The "server-brave-search" MCP server is designed to integrate with the Brave Search API, enabling AI models to perform both web and local searches. Detailed features include:

- **Web Search**: This allows for general queries, news, and articles, with options for pagination and freshness controls. Users can specify the number of results per page (up to 20) and offset for pagination (up to 9), ensuring flexibility in retrieving information.
- **Local Search**: Focused on finding businesses, restaurants, and services, it provides detailed information and automatically falls back to web search if no local results are found, enhancing reliability.
- **Flexible Filtering**: Users can control result types, safety levels, and content freshness, tailoring searches to specific needs.
- **Smart Fallbacks**: Ensures continuity by switching to web search when local search yields no results, improving user experience.

The server supports tools such as `brave_web_search` and `brave_local_search`, each with defined inputs:
- `brave_web_search`: Requires a `query` (string), optional `count` (number, max 20), and `offset` (number, max 9).
- `brave_local_search`: Requires a `query` (string) and optional `count` (number, max 20), with automatic fallback to web search if needed.

#### Technical Requirements and Setup
To utilize the "server-brave-search" MCP server, an API key from Brave Search is necessary. Users can sign up at [Brave Search API](https://brave.com/search/api/) and choose a plan, with a free tier offering 2,000 queries per month, suitable for initial exploration. Setup instructions include:
- Docker-based installation: Run with environment variable `BRAVE_API_KEY` set to your API key.
- Node.js-based installation: Use `npx -y @modelcontextprotocol/server-brave-search` with the same environment variable.

These details are available in the repository at [modelcontextprotocol/servers](https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/servers/tree/main/src/brave-search) and the dedicated page at [Awesome MCP Servers](https://mcpservers.org/servers/modelcontextprotocol/brave-search).

#### Comparative Context
The analysis also considered other search-related MCP servers to ensure accuracy, such as `pskill9/web-search` (using Google results), `nickclyde/duckduckgo-mcp-server` (using DuckDuckGo), and `kshern/mcp-tavily` (Tavily AI search API). However, the user’s specific mention of "Brave Search" aligns with the "server-brave-search" server, distinguishing it from alternatives. This server’s focus on both web and local search, with integration into the MCP ecosystem, positions it uniquely for AI-driven search tasks.

#### Practical Applications and Relevance
Given the current date (April 24, 2025), and Dubai’s tech-savvy environment as mentioned in prior discussions, the "server-brave-search" MCP server is particularly relevant for businesses and developers in the region seeking to enhance AI capabilities with robust search functionalities. Its integration with n8n, as discussed in earlier conversations, could facilitate automated workflows for lead generation or market research, leveraging Brave’s privacy-focused search engine.

#### Summary Table of Key Attributes

| Attribute                | Details                                                                 |
|--------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| Name                     | server-brave-search                                                    |
| Purpose                  | Web and local search using Brave Search API                            |
| Features                 | Pagination, filtering, local search with fallback, safety controls     |
| API Key Requirement      | Yes, sign up at [Brave Search API](https://brave.com/search/api/)      |
| Free Tier                | 2,000 queries/month                                                    |
| Supported Tools          | brave_web_search, brave_local_search                                   |
| Repository Location      | [modelcontextprotocol/servers](https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/servers) |

#### Conclusion
The Brave Search MCP tool, identified as the "server-brave-search" MCP server, is a robust integration with the Brave Search API, enabling AI models to perform web and local searches with advanced features. Its inclusion in the [punkpeye/awesome-mcp-servers](https://github.com/punkpeye/awesome-mcp-servers) list and detailed documentation at [Awesome MCP Servers](https://mcpservers.org/servers/modelcontextprotocol/brave-search) underscores its relevance for developers and businesses, especially in a tech hub like Dubai as of April 24, 2025.

### **HUMAN**:
what mcp to streamline building with n8n ? one that does all the configurations for me ?

### **ASSISTANT**:
### Answer to "What MCP to streamline building with n8n? One that does all the configurations for me?"

Unfortunately, there isn’t a single MCP (Model Context Protocol) tool that fully automates all configurations for building workflows with n8n. However, you can streamline the process significantly by using the **n8n-nodes-mcp** package, which simplifies integrating MCP functionality into n8n. While it doesn’t handle *all* configurations automatically, it reduces manual effort, and you can further simplify things by leveraging community resources. Here’s a complete breakdown of the solution:

#### Recommended MCP Tool: n8n-nodes-mcp
The **n8n-nodes-mcp** package is a community node for n8n that allows you to connect your n8n workflows to MCP servers. With this tool, you can:
- Execute tools hosted on MCP servers.
- List available tools and their details.
- Integrate MCP functionality directly into your n8n workflows via a pre-built node.

While it requires some initial setup (like installing the node and providing API keys), it eliminates the need to build custom integrations from scratch, making it the closest option to streamlining your workflow-building process.

#### How to Use It
1. **Set Up Your n8n Instance**: Install n8n on your laptop (it’s lightweight and can run locally or in the cloud).
2. **Install n8n-nodes-mcp**: Add this package to your n8n instance via the community nodes feature. You can find installation instructions in the [n8n-nodes-mcp documentation](https://github.com/nerding-io/n8n-nodes-mcp).
3. **Configure the Node**: Add the MCP node to your workflow, input your MCP server credentials (e.g., API key), and connect it to other n8n nodes as needed.

#### Going Further: Minimizing Manual Configuration
Since you’re looking for a tool that “does all the configurations for me,” here’s how to reduce manual work even more:
- **Pre-Built Workflows**: The n8n community shares ready-made workflows on platforms like the [n8n community forum](https://n8n.io/community/) or [GitHub](https://github.com/search?q=n8n+workflows). Look for workflows that already include the n8n-nodes-mcp node configured for common use cases. You can import these into your n8n instance and tweak them to fit your needs.
- **n8n-mcp-server**: This is an MCP server specifically designed to manage n8n workflows programmatically. It lets you list, execute, and manage workflows via the MCP interface. While it requires an initial n8n setup, it can automate ongoing workflow management once configured. Check the [MCP Servers Hub](https://mcpservers.org) for details.

#### Step-by-Step Simplified Approach
Here’s a practical plan to get started quickly:
1. **Install n8n**: Download and run n8n on your laptop (instructions at [n8n.io](https://n8n.io)).
2. **Add n8n-nodes-mcp**: Install it through n8n’s interface under “Community Nodes.”
3. **Find a Workflow**: Search the n8n community or GitHub for a workflow with MCP integration that matches your goal (e.g., automating social media tasks or data processing).
4. **Import and Run**: Import the workflow into n8n, connect it to your MCP server (if required), and test it.
5. **Optional – Use n8n-mcp-server**: If you need to manage workflows programmatically, set up this server for automation.

#### Limitations
No MCP tool fully eliminates configuration at this time. The n8n-nodes-mcp package and pre-built workflows get you close, but you’ll still need to:
- Install and set up n8n.
- Configure the MCP node with server details.
- Adjust imported workflows to your specific use case.

If you’re short on time or technical expertise, you could explore hiring someone to set this up for you, though that depends on your budget.

#### Why This Works for You
Since you mentioned having a laptop, a phone, and social media access with a goal to make money in Dubai within a week, this approach lets you quickly build automated workflows (e.g., social media posting, lead generation) with minimal setup. The n8n-nodes-mcp package connects you to powerful MCP tools, and community workflows save you from starting from scratch.

#### Final Recommendation
Use the **n8n-nodes-mcp** package as your MCP tool to streamline building with n8n. Pair it with pre-built community workflows and, if needed, the **n8n-mcp-server** for management. This combination offers the easiest path to get started without deep manual configuration.

For more help, check out:
- [n8n-nodes-mcp documentation](https://github.com/nerding-io/n8n-nodes-mcp)
- [n8n Community](https://n8n.io/community/)
- [MCP Servers Hub](https://mcpservers.org)

This setup won’t do *everything* for you, but it’s the most automated solution available for n8n and MCP integration!
